text.content:
Book of the Wider World — p.144-149

Two days northwest of Stonetop, the rolling Flats (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.7Rj5pjaWepSJPAms]{126}) give way to hills, climbing up into the Whitefang Mountains (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JJlDjo038UgWzFTy]{480}). The Highway (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.FEIURyGVNdxdU9vY]{270}) cuts through these hills, leading to Barrier Pass (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.at3jDszAWvz0csBO]{26}). To the east, the hills wrap around the Great Wood (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{200}).

The Foothills are rugged and forest-strewn, crisscrossed with streams, dotted with gorges and ponds and small lakes. In ages past, it was a borderland between the Rime Lords (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.DeWQFy1cWoLRvoU5]{316}) and the Green Lords (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.gnP4Q10EDePdvgqz]{210}), and later it was the extent of the Barrow Builders’ reach (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.Qir4rs4BUipTSyFQ]{34}). Nowadays, it is mostly uninhabited, but both Stonetop and Barrier Pass come here to harvest timber.

Impressions

Always

  • Hills rising up from the Flats, mountains looming behind them
  • Young thin trees on the southernmost slopes, getting bigger and older as you press into the hills
  • Echoes of birdsong, beast-bellows, tumbling rocks, raised voices

Spring

  • Thin branches drooping with fresh green leaves
  • Mud slipping and squishing underfoot
  • Surprising chill of shadowed vales, where snow still lingers

Summer

  • Muggy, humid heat
  • Sweat stinging your eyes as you hump up another slope
  • Cold clear water from a gurgling stream, tasting better than anything

Autumn

  • Slopes turned red and orange and yellow, then black and brown and gray
  • Plump squirrels and other critters, busily foraging for winter
  • Cold, clammy mist lasting well into the day

Winter

  • Waist-deep snow drifts in the valleys
  • Frozen-over streams, water gurgling ominously from below
  • Squeak of trees, swaying in the cold wind
  • Short, magical twilight as the sun drops below the mountains

Questions

  • What striking difference do you notice between the trees here and those in the Great Wood?
  • Who typically leads Stonetop’s logging trips up to the Foothills?
  • What dread beast or monster is said to lurk in the Foothills?
  • What is said to draw its ire?
  • Who claims to have encountered it? When, and how so?
  • Why do you/don’t you believe them?
  • What mundane dangers up there worry you more than some legendary beast?

Hooks

  • A logging expedition goes missing
  • Loggers stumble upon a ruin or corrupted site
  • Someone went missing on a logging trip years ago, and now they reappear
  • Heavy rains wash out a stretch of the Highway, blocking access to good timber
  • An adventurer claims to know the location of an old barrow in the Foothills, said to be laden with gold and other treasure

Logging trips

A typical logging trip generates a wagonload of timber (Value 2), and requires:

  • A wagon and two horses;
  • ~20 able-bodied workers; and
  • Pulling Together over 9-12 days (for travel and work)

Trips traditionally take place in autumn, after the harvest is complete and only if there are no more-pressing needs in town.

The Palisade steading improvement requires ~1,000 logs of good timber (Value 3). A wagonload of timber (Value 2) contains ~40-50 logs, meaning you need ~20-25 wagonloads. To gather that much timber:

  • You need ~20 able-bodied workers, a wagon, and two horses working for the course of a season
  • You must talk to a few town elders, and get their support
  • You risk accidents and attacks
  • You must spend 1 Surplus (to feed and equip the loggers) and reduce Fortunes by 1 (because of so many people and resources being away for so long)
  • The steading must Pull Together over the course of the season

Steading improvement

PERMANENT LOGGING CAMP Suppose we built a few cabins and kept loggers up there year-round?

Requires all of the following:

  • Two or three permanent dwellings, just off the Highway
  • Supplies, furnishings, and tools for those dwellings (Value 2)
  • 5-6 capable adults, willing and able to relocate and log year-round
  • An extra wagon (Value 3) and extra horse or mule (Value 3) to haul timber to and from Stonetop Four seasons of operation, each generating a wagonload of timber (Value 2)

When you mark all the requirements, add “Logging: timber, wood” to the resources list and increase Fortunes by 1.

Henceforth, as long as the camp is in operation, the steading has a ready supply of timber for improvements and other projects, and it consumes 1 less Surplus than usual in winter. However, the logging camp consumes 1 Surplus every summer or else it ceases operation.

Terrain

Pick 1 or combine 2, or have someone roll the Die of Fate (d12). 1d12 terrain

@DrawTable[Compendium.stonetop.wonder-tables.RollTable.yTYV5hXz8D6DSoKR]{1d12}Terrain

1Large pond/small lake
2Creek, stream, gulley
3Steep slope: barren, treacherous, unstable
4Dense thicket/brush
5-6Swath/copse of one type of tree
7-8Gentle slope or valley
9Steep slope: forested, overgrown
10Sheer rock face, bluff, rocky outcrop
11Clearing, meadow, burn site
12Cave, cavern, grotto

If you like, roll 1d6 for size/scope/intensity.

Discoveries

For any of these, pick 1 or have someone roll the Die of Fate.

@DrawTable[Compendium.stonetop.wonder-tables.RollTable.aFgG4R2Yb1gk5aag]{1d6}Discovery

1A change of terrain (page 147), and roll again
2A chance for insight into a threat or danger (e.g. tracks that reveal numbers, whereabouts, etc.)
3folk from barrier pass (next column)
4Some other encounter (next column)
5A point of interest (see below)
6Useful or valuable flora (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.y0RKxmPHaMCuP9r6]{458})

@DrawTable[Compendium.stonetop.wonder-tables.RollTable.KRHocI9rfmYnFnnA]{1d6}Folk from barrier pass

1A war party of 4-8 guardians and/ or monks, hunting something dangerous
2A patrol of 2-4 guardians, led by a White Crystal monk
3A monk and/or novice, on their own to study/practice/meditate/ make pilgrimage
4A logging crew (4-12 layfolk, plus a few guardians)
5-61-4 layfolk hunting or foraging

Consider having someone roll the Die of Fate for their disposition. See page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.at3jDszAWvz0csBO]{33} for guardian, monk, and novice stats.

@DrawTable[Compendium.stonetop.wonder-tables.RollTable.e2GIDcMQC62TOXHQ]{1d6}Encounter

1A chimera (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.gnP4Q10EDePdvgqz]{218}), wary or indifferent but not outright hostile
2A Fae (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.YMV8KIodnoZL71Jl]{94}), curious or indifferent
3A tulpa (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.DeWQFy1cWoLRvoU5]{324}), likely tethered to a nearby Rime Lord site
4A spirit of the wild (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.HOWpYxmR8Q44qS3E]{356}), curious or indifferent
5A dangerous beast—like a boar (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{204}), cave bear (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{205}), cougar (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{206}), ice drake (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JJlDjo038UgWzFTy]{491}), mountain apes (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JJlDjo038UgWzFTy]{494}), or wolves (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{209})—wary or indifferent, but easily provoked
6Game: good eating but prone to bolt (deer, rabbits, pheasant, etc.)

@DrawTable[Compendium.stonetop.wonder-tables.RollTable.fYVMvHrTZiCO3nJl]{1d6}Point of interest

1A place corrupted by the Things Below (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.HurhcWKZBn60Vcq3]{412})
2A place haunted by death or the undying (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.sNLYfvJEFM1v463y]{72})
3A site or artifact of the Barrow Builders (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.Qir4rs4BUipTSyFQ]{34})
4A ruin or artifact of the Green Lords (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.gnP4Q10EDePdvgqz]{210}) or a Fae domain (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.YMV8KIodnoZL71Jl]{102})
5A ruin or artifact of the Rime Lords (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.DeWQFy1cWoLRvoU5]{316}) or their human disciples
6A place sacred to the spirits of the wild (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.HOWpYxmR8Q44qS3E]{356})

Dangers

Hazards

  • Getting lost among the hills and trees
  • Treacherous terrain (loose footing, scree, slippery slopes, potholes, deadfall, etc.)
  • Flash flooding, especially in ravines and valleys
  • Harsh weather, especially rain and snow
  • Something tall to climb, and reason to climb it
  • Hazardous growth (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.gnP4Q10EDePdvgqz]{219}), from the time of the Green Lords

Monsters

  • Ornery beasts: boars (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{204}), cave bears (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{204}), ice drakes (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JJlDjo038UgWzFTy]{491}), mountain apes (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JJlDjo038UgWzFTy]{494}), wisents (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{209})
  • Predators: butcherbirds (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{204}), cougars (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{206}), feathered drakes (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{207}), or wolves (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{209})
  • Spirits of the wild (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.HOWpYxmR8Q44qS3E]{356})
  • Fae (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.YMV8KIodnoZL71Jl]{94})
  • A hagr (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JjyXyicr58qim0bV]{208}) or two, come up from the Great Wood
  • Tulpas (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.DeWQFy1cWoLRvoU5]{324}), tied to Rime Lord sites or perhaps to Barrier Pass
  • Chimerae (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.gnP4Q10EDePdvgqz]{218}) and other remnants of the Green Lords
  • Ghosts (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.sNLYfvJEFM1v463y]{76}) or revenants (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.sNLYfvJEFM1v463y]{80}), lurking near old barrows, tombs, or battlefields
  • A copse of dool trees (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.sNLYfvJEFM1v463y]{73}) and its resident spirits
  • A bhoka (page @UUID[Compendium.stonetop.wider-world-and-other-wonders.JournalEntry.JJlDjo038UgWzFTy]{490}), come down from the mountains
  • Whatever dangerous beast or monster your players tell you dwells in these hills
